scenario: Draft a plaint for recovery of Rs.15,00,000 given as a hand loan. The plaintiff Ram Kumar, aged 45, resident of Jayanagar, Bengaluru, advanced Rs.15,00,000 to the defendant Suresh Patel, aged 38, resident of Koramangala, Bengaluru, on 15.03.2024 via NEFT bank transfer (UTR: AXIB20240315123456). There was no written agreement. The defendant acknowledged the loan via WhatsApp messages. Despite repeated oral demands and a legal notice dated 01.07.2024, the defendant has failed to repay. The plaintiff claims interest at 12% per annum from the date of default (20.06.2024). File in City Civil Court, Bengaluru.

scorecard.detail: At least one statutory section cited

draft_text:
IN THE COURT OF THE CITY CIVIL COURT
AT BENGALURU

O.S. No. __________ / 2025

Ram Kumar,
Aged about 45 years,
Occupation: {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Jayanagar, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... PLAINTIFF

                    VERSUS

Suresh Patel,
Aged about 38 years,
Occupation: {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}},
Residing at: Koramangala, Bengaluru.

                                                        ... DEFENDANT

SUIT FOR RECOVERY OF MONEY LENT / ADVANCE PAID WITH INTEREST AND COSTS

MOST RESPECTFULLY SHOWETH:

JURISDICTION

1. The Plaintiff is a person sui juris and competent to institute the present suit. The Defendant is also a person sui juris and can be sued.

2. This Hon'ble Court has territorial jurisdiction to try and entertain the present suit inasmuch as the cause of action arose within the local limits of the jurisdiction of this Hon'ble Court at Bengaluru, and the Defendant carries on business / resides within such territorial limits.

3. This Hon'ble Court has pecuniary jurisdiction to try the present suit, the suit being valued at Rs. {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE}}/- (Rupees {{TOTAL_SUIT_VALUE_WORDS}} Only), which is within the pecuniary limits prescribed for this Hon'ble Court.

LIMITATION

4. The present suit is within the period of limitation prescribed under Article 19 of the Schedule to the Limitation Act, 1963, which provides a period of three years from the date of accrual of the cause of action. The suit is filed well within the prescribed limitation period.

FACTS OF THE CASE

5. The Plaintiff is a {{PLAINTIFF_OCCUPATION}} by occupation and resides at Jayanagar, Bengaluru. The Plaintiff is acquainted with the Defendant, who is a {{DEFENDANT_OCCUPATION}} by occupation and resides at Koramangala, Bengaluru.

6. On or about 15.03.2024, the Defendant approached the Plaintiff requesting financial assistance of Rs. 15,00,000/- (Rupees Fifteen Lakhs Only) as a hand loan for his personal requirements.

7. Acting on the request and assurance of the Defendant, the Plaintiff advanced the sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- to the Defendant on 15.03.2024 through NEFT bank transfer. The transaction bears the Unique Transaction Reference (UTR) Number AXIB20240315123456. A copy of the bank record evidencing the transfer is annexed herewith as Annexure A.

8. Though no formal written loan agreement was executed between the parties, the Defendant acknowledged the receipt of the said loan amount and the liability to repay the same through electronic messages exchanged on the WhatsApp platform. Copies of the relevant WhatsApp messages are annexed herewith as Annexure B.

9. The transaction was purely a civil loan advanced out of friendship and acquaintance, and there exists no commercial or banking relationship between the parties other than the stated debt.

BREACH & PARTICULARS OF DEFAULT

10. It was mutually understood and agreed between the parties that the Defendant would repay the loan amount within a reasonable time. However, the Defendant failed to adhere to the implied terms of repayment.

11. The Defendant committed a default in repayment of the loan amount on 20.06.2024. Despite the lapse of time, the Defendant has not discharged his liability.

12. The Plaintiff made repeated oral demands upon the Defendant to repay the outstanding dues. The Defendant merely acknowledged the debt but failed to make any payment towards the principal amount.

13. Due to the persistent failure of the Defendant to repay the loan, the Plaintiff was compelled to issue a legal notice dated 01.07.2024 through his counsel, calling upon the Defendant to repay the dues. The Defendant received the said notice but has failed to comply with the demand made therein. A copy of the legal notice is annexed herewith as Annexure C.

DAMAGES

14. The Defendant is legally liable to repay the principal loan amount of Rs. 15,00,000/- advanced on 15.03.2024. The Plaintiff has suffered financial loss due to the wrongful retention of the said money by the Defendant.

15. The Plaintiff is entitled to claim interest on the outstanding amount at the rate of 12% per annum from the date of default, i.e., 20.06.2024, till the date of actual realization. The Plaintiff claims interest pendente lite and future interest at the same rate.

16. The total claim comprises the principal sum of Rs. 15,00,000/- along with accrued interest and costs of the litigation. The Plaintiff has paid the requisite court fee on the claimed amount as per the Court Fees Act.

LEGAL BASIS

17. The Defendant borrowed / received a sum of Rs. {{CLAIM_AMOUNT}}/- from the Plaintiff and is liable to repay the same with interest under Section 70 read with Section 73 of the Indian Contract Act, 1872.

18. The present suit is maintainable under Section 9 of the Code of Civil Procedure, 1908.

CAUSE OF ACTION

19. The cause of action for the present suit first arose on 20.06.2024 when the Defendant committed the breach / wrongful act giving rise to t
